qtype: expanding brackets, factorisation by common factor
tex:
(a) 
Expand and simplify.
\newline

\[
5(x - 2) + 3(x - 7)
\]

Factorise.
\newline

\[
4a^2 + 16a
\]

content.content:
Expand and simplify.
\newline

\[
5(x - 2) + 3(x - 7)
\]

Factorise.
\newline

\[
4a^2 + 16a
\]

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}

\part Expand and simplify.

\[
5(x - 2) + 3(x - 7)
\]

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\part Factorise.

\[
4a^2 + 16a
\]

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}

qtype: percentage increase, fraction of a quantity, percentage of a quantity

\newline

The shop gives $\dfrac{2}{5}$ of the profit from May to a charity.
\newline

Find the amount the shop gives to the charity.
\newline

The shop spends 38\% of the profit from June on a fridge.
\newline

Find the amount the shop spends on the fridge.
\newline

qtype: multi-step calculation, forming linear equations, solving linear equations
tex:
The table gives information about the costs of hiring bikes.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Type of bike & Cost for first day & Cost for each extra day \\
\hline
Road & \$25 & \$20 \\
\hline
Mountain & \$40 & \$35 \\
\hline
Electric & \$70 & \$50 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) 
Work out the cost of hiring 2 road bikes for 3 days.
\newline

The cost, $M$, of hiring a mountain bike for $d$ days can be written as $M = 35d + 5$.
\newline

(i)  Write a formula for the cost, $E$, of hiring an electric bike for $d$ days.
\newline

 The cost of hiring an electric bike for 6 days is the same as the cost of hiring a mountain bike for $d$ days.
\newline

Find the value of $d$.
\newline

qtype: ordering numbers in standard form, ratio calculation, rounding, rates
tex:

These are the distances above the surface of the Earth of five satellites, $A$, $B$, $C$, $D$ and $E$.

\begin{tabular}{ccccc}
$A$ & $B$ & $C$ & $D$ & $E$ \\
$35\,800$ & $7.8 \times 10^2$ & $1.5 \times 10^6$ & 535 & $2 \times 10^4$ \\
\end{tabular}
(i)  Write these distances in order, starting with the shortest.
\newline

... , ... , ... , ... , ...

 *shortest*

 The radius of Earth is $6370\,\text{km}$.
\newline

Satellite $A$ is $k$ times further from the **centre** of Earth than satellite $D$.
\newline

Show that $k = 6.11$ correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

A satellite travels at a speed of $27\,000\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Find the distance the satellite travels in 95 minutes.
\newline

\newline

Convert this speed into m/s.
\newline
